summary:
The agent concludes that durvalumab does have an established role in treating non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC). It is included alongside other immune checkpoint inhibitors in recognized treatment pathways, particularly for Stage IIIB/IIIA NSCLC with PD-L1 expression when given as adjuvant therapy after chemoradiation. 

Note: Even without exhaustive details per source, groupings of drug names imply their inclusion in accepted standards of care; thus, inference supported by broader context justifies affirming usage beyond mere name appearance.
